task_completion.description: Verify that the bcWebCam application is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Options dialog of bcWebCam has been opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate Options dialog window for bcWebCam is visible, typically accessed via the gear icon in the bottom toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Append terminating character to barcode data' section is located.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Append terminating character to barcode data' is visible within the Options dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify that the '<TAB>' radio button is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The radio button next to '<TAB>' is filled/checked in the 'Append terminating character to barcode data' section.

task_completion.description: Verify that the OK button is clicked to apply the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OK button in the Options dialog is highlighted, clicked, or the Options dialog disappears in subsequent frames after the '<TAB>' option was selected.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to make the change.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show mouse movements, clicks, and GUI windows (bcWebCam Options dialog) rather than a terminal window editing configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being interacted with is clearly bcWebCam, not a text editor, script, or mock-up window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use command-line tools to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal or command prompt windows are open showing commands that modify bcWebCam settings directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The default configuration uses '{ENTER}'. The target state requires the user to change this to TAB via the bcWebCam UI Options dialog. Real-world product EAN barcodes and URLs are placed on the desktop by the environment scripts, but the task does not require scanning them.
